hint.serverNameHelper: A short name that identifies this integration
hint.descriptionHelper: Optional — helps team members understand this server's purpose
hint.tagsHelper: Optional — used to filter and organize servers
hint.tagsPlaceholder: Press Enter or comma to add…
hint.baseUrlOverrideHint: Leave blank to use the URL declared in the spec.
hint.baseUrlCollectionHint: Override the base URL from the collection. Leave blank to use the collection URL.
hint.baseUrlApiHint: The root address of the external API.
hint.specNameHint: The server name will be taken from the spec's info.title. You can rename it after creation.
hint.toolsEditableHint: Tool parameters, descriptions, and output schemas are editable from the server detail page after creation.
hint.mcpKeysHint: MCP API keys and rate limiting are available in the server settings after creation.
hint.credentialsHint: Use tokens with minimum required scope. Credentials are stored in the database.
hint.readOnlyWarning: Use a read-only user with access limited to only the tables the AI needs.
hint.mongoReadOnlyWarning: Use an account scoped to only the collections the AI needs.
hint.dynamoIamHint: Prefer an IAM user with least-privilege permissions — read-only access to specific tables.
hint.firestoreRoleHint: Create a service account with Cloud Datastore User role (read-only) for minimal permissions.
hint.invalidUrl: Invalid URL — include the protocol (e.g. https://api.example.com)
hint.dragOpenApi: Drag your OpenAPI / Swagger spec here
hint.skipToEmpty: Optional — skip to start with an empty server
hint.toolsAutoGenerated: Tools will be generated automatically from this spec
hint.postmanImported: Postman collection will be imported as MCP tools
hint.dragPostman: Drag your Postman Collection v2 here
hint.discovered: Discovered {{count}} tools from the spec at this URL.
hint.emptyServerHint: The server will be created empty. Add tools manually from the server detail page.
hint.configureUrl: Configure this URL in Claude Desktop, Cursor, or any compatible MCP client.
hint.authHeaderRequired: This server requires authentication — send the header auth: <your-key>
hint.scanQr: Scan with a mobile device to transfer the URL
hint.noAuthRequired: No authentication required — ready to use immediately.

auth.credentialsHint: Credentials attached to every outgoing request. Never exposed to the AI.
auth.grpcCredentialsHint: Credentials sent as metadata headers with every gRPC call. Never exposed to the AI.
auth.noCredentials: No credentials attached. Use for public APIs.
auth.oauthFlow: Uses client_credentials flow. Token renewed automatically.
auth.customHeadersHint: Add HTTP headers to every request (e.g. X-Tenant-Id).
auth.comingSoon: Full {{type}} execution support is coming soon. The server configuration will be saved and activated when the adapter is released.

docs.authWarning: This server requires authentication — send the header auth: <your-key>

share.title: MCP Server URL
share.urlDescription: This is the address you'll paste into your AI client's MCP configuration.
share.claudeDesktopHint: Edit the file ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json (macOS) or %APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json (Windows) and add:
share.cursorHint: Open Settings → Features → MCP Servers and click Add new MCP server. Choose HTTP transport, paste the URL below and save.
share.anyClientHint: Point your client to this URL using Streamable HTTP transport.
share.anyClientAuthHint: Include the header auth: <your-api-key> in every request.
share.requiresKey: This server requires an API key. Contact the person who shared this link to obtain your personal key.
share.poweredBy: Powered by Arthur MCP · This page is inspired by the OpenAPI / Swagger UI.
share.authorizeDescription: Authorize simulator requests with the same credentials an MCP client would use.
share.apiKeyHelper: The key stays in your browser and is sent as the auth header for simulator requests.
share.oauthClientCredentialsHelper: The simulator exchanges these credentials for an access token and sends Authorization: Bearer <token>.
share.oauthTokenMissing: The token endpoint did not return an access token.
share.authRequiredToTest: Enter the MCP API key before running this simulation.
share.noSimulatorInputs: This operation does not require input.
share.referenceDescription: Public, read-only documentation for this MCP server, including tools, resources, prompts, descriptions, and output schemas.
share.noTools: No tools are exposed by this server.
share.noResources: No resources are exposed by this server.
share.noPrompts: No prompts are exposed by this server.
share.mcpProtocolDescription: Standard JSON-RPC 2.0 methods exposed by every MCP server. Use these to inspect the server's capabilities programmatically or to test connectivity.
share.mcpInitializeDesc: Exchanges protocol version and capabilities between client and server. Always the first call in an MCP session.
share.mcpToolsListDesc: Returns the full list of available tools with their names, descriptions, and JSON Schema input definitions.
share.mcpResourcesListDesc: Returns the list of static and dynamic resources this server exposes, each identified by a unique URI.
share.mcpResourcesTemplatesListDesc: Returns URI templates for parameterised resources. Clients substitute variables to construct concrete resource URIs.
share.mcpPromptsListDesc: Returns the list of prompt templates available on this server, with their names, descriptions, and required arguments.

upload.dragHint: Drag your Swagger / OpenAPI file here

help.intro: The API Upload page lets you create a fully configured MCP server by importing an existing API definition file, instead of creating tools one by one by hand.
help.howToUseStep1Prefix: Drop your file (or click to browse). Supported:
help.howToUseStep2Suffix: Use this when the spec declares a staging URL but you want to point Arthur at production.
help.howToUseStep3Suffix: Arthur reads every path and operation in the spec, creates a server, and generates one tool per endpoint.
help.generatedTitle: What Arthur generates from the spec:
help.generatedItem1Prefix: A server named after the spec's
help.generatedItem2Prefix: One tool per operation, named from the
help.generatedItem4: Input parameters (path, query, body) mapped to the MCP tool's parameter schema.
help.tipBody: The richer your spec's descriptions, the better the AI will understand when and how to use each tool. After import you can edit any tool individually from the server's Tools tab.
help.successBody: A success message shows the server name and number of generated tools. Open the server, review its Tools, confirm the base URL and source authentication, then make a test call before connecting an AI client.
help.troubleshootingTitle: If the import does not look right:
help.troubleshootingFormat: If the file is rejected, validate that it is OpenAPI 3.x or Swagger 2.0 and valid YAML or JSON with a .yaml, .yml, or .json extension.
help.troubleshootingUrl: If generated calls target the wrong host, import again with the correct Base URL override or correct the connection after import.
help.troubleshootingDescriptions: If tools have unclear names or parameters, improve operationId, summary, description, and parameter definitions in the spec, or edit the generated tools before use.

graphql.endpointDescription: The URL of your GraphQL API. Schema discovery will be performed via introspection.
graphql.schemaSourceDescription: Choose how to load the GraphQL schema.
graphql.autoIntrospectDescription: Query the endpoint's introspection API to discover all operations automatically.
graphql.uploadSdlDescription: Provide a .graphql or .gql SDL schema file manually.
graphql.operationsHint: Operations discovered from the schema will each become an MCP tool with typed inputs.

grpc.serviceAddressDescription: The host and port of the gRPC server.
grpc.protoDefinitionDescription: Upload your .proto file. Each RPC method will become an MCP tool.

mongodb.connectionDescription: Connection URI including credentials and database name.

redis.connectionDescription: Connection details for your Redis instance.

dynamodb.connectionDescription: AWS credentials to access your DynamoDB tables.
dynamodb.customEndpointHint: Leave blank to use the standard AWS endpoint. Use for DynamoDB Local.

elasticsearch.connectionDescription: URL and credentials for your Elasticsearch cluster.
elasticsearch.clusterUrlHint: Full URL including protocol and port
elasticsearch.orBasicAuth: Or use Basic Auth — fill username and password below.

snowflake.connectionDescription: Account and credentials for your Snowflake data warehouse.

firestore.connectionDescription: Google Cloud project and service account credentials.
firestore.serviceAccountHint: Paste the content of your service account key file

help.intro: A server turns a connected data source into an MCP endpoint that an AI client can use. The source can be a REST or GraphQL API, a database, or static content. Open a server card to configure what the AI may call or read.
help.step1: Choose New server, start from a template, or import an OpenAPI/Swagger file. Select the source type and enter the connection details requested by the creation flow.
help.step2: Open the new server and define Operations for the source, then expose the capabilities the AI needs as Tools, Resources, or Prompts. REST imports may generate tools from the specification for you.
help.step3: Configure the source credential where required. This is how Arthur connects to the source and is separate from the Access Key or OAuth configuration that protects the MCP endpoint.
help.step4: In Connect, copy the MCP endpoint URL and configure access for the AI client. Use an Access Key or OAuth when the endpoint must not be public.
help.step5: Connect the AI client and ask it to use one enabled Tool. Arthur records the call in the server's Activity section so you can confirm the result.
help.cardIndicators: Card indicators — what each element on a server card means:
help.colouredDot: Source icon: identifies the kind of source connected to the server, such as REST, GraphQL, database, or static content. It describes the connection type, not its health.
help.activePaused: Status chip: Active means the server is available; Error indicates a saved server state that needs review; Paused means MCP calls are temporarily blocked until you resume it. Open the server and check Activity for runtime call health.
help.toolCount: Capability counts: show how many Tools, Resources, and Prompts are configured. A zero Tool count means the AI has no callable action on this server, although it may still read Resources or use Prompts.
help.tags: Tags: custom labels you define for organisation and filtering. Use them to group servers by team, environment (production, staging), or the API type they connect to. Click a tag on the filter bar above to show only servers with that tag.
help.success: The server appears in this list, at least one intended capability is enabled, the AI client connects through the URL shown in Connect, and a test call appears in Activity without an error.
help.troubleshootingNoTools: If the client connects but cannot do anything, confirm that the server has an enabled Tool and that its name, description, and parameters are clear.
help.troubleshootingErrors: For 401 or 403 errors, distinguish source authentication from MCP access: check the source credential, then the Access Key or OAuth configuration in Connect.
help.troubleshootingMissing: If a server or action is missing, clear the search/tag filter and confirm that your role has the required view or edit permission.
help.newServerHint: Deleting a server also removes its configured capabilities and breaks active client connections. Duplicate a server when you need a safe starting point for a variation.
